title: Can I measure distance with breakpoints?

content.text: Yes. You can measure distance with multiple breakpoints directly in the map.

items: Select “Draw and measure” in the menu.
Choose the line tool and click “Show measurements”.
Click in the map where you want to start the measurement.
Click additional points to create breakpoints.
Finish the measurement with a double-click.

content.text: The total length will be displayed in the map where the line ends.

title: Can I delete individual objects when drawing?

content.text: Yes. The drawing tool allows you to select and delete individual objects.

items: Click the pointer icon in the upper-left corner of the drawing tool.
Click the object you want to delete.
When the object becomes dashed it is selected.
Click the trash icon to delete the selected object.

title: Which search criteria are supported in the search field?

content.text: In addition to place names and addresses, you can search using combinations of municipality name/number and property identifiers.
Examples:

title: How do I search for coordinates?

content.text: The search field supports several coordinate formats.
Geographic coordinates:
UTM coordinates:

If the coordinate values are larger than 180 (which would normally indicate degrees), the search engine assumes UTM33 coordinates, which is the default coordinate system used in the map, for example: 487560,7442215.
You can also specify the coordinate system using an EPSG code, for example: 306461.46,6885442.27@EPSG:25832.

title: How do I share a map view?

items: A unique URL will be generated automatically.
The link contains your current map view and can be shared with others.

title: How do I share a map with drawings?

content.text: To include drawings when sharing the map, you must first save the drawing.

items: Click the save icon at the bottom of the drawing menu.
A unique URL will be generated.
Share the map using “Share” in the menu or copy the URL from the search field.

title: Can I print in formats other than A4?

content.text: The map can be printed in both A3 and A4 formats.

title: What are the terms of use?

content.text: Kartverket’s terms of use are available on kartverket.no.

title: Is there a legend for the map layers?

content.text: Detailed map symbology for standard background maps is available at
Geonorge
When thematic maps are enabled, you can view the legend by clicking the “Legend” button in the lower-right corner.
On mobile, you can view the legend by tapping the info icon in the side menu.

title: How often is the map updated?

content.text: Update frequency depends on the map layer. The standard color map is updated weekly, while other layers are updated at varying intervals.

title: Where can I find property information?

content.text: In Norgeskart you can view property boundaries and basic property information.

items: Click on a property in the map.
A result panel will appear in the upper-right corner.
Select “Property information” to view municipality number, farm number and usage number.
Click “Show on map” to highlight the property boundary.
Click “Show more” to open additional details in the Property Register.

title: How do I activate full screen mode?

content.text: The toolbar in the lower-right corner of the map includes several functions such as zoom, rotation, location and full screen mode.

title: How do I view an elevation profile?

content.text: The elevation profile tool is available under “Create map” in the main menu.

items: Click a starting point in the map.
Click where you want the measurement to end.

title: Why can’t I print aerial imagery?

content.text: Printing aerial imagery (orthophotos) is currently not supported. A solution is being worked on for the future.

title: Hide the menu with the keyboard

content.text: You can hide and show the menu by pressing the 'U' key on your keyboard.
